taskName: Fix batch upload homepage state so completion appears only after the whole batch finishes
goal: Fix FileEasy batch upload state reporting so the APK service homepage stays in the receiving/uploading state until every file in the selected batch has completed.
nonGoals: Do not redesign the upload page visual layout.
Do not add remote admin or file-management functionality.
Do not change allowed file types, chunk size, 4GB limit, or password rules.
Do not implement upload history entry, cross-network guidance, or app icon fixes in this task.
Do not reintroduce player, monitor, discovery, or public access behavior.

acceptanceChecks.text: When a user selects multiple valid files and starts upload, the APK service homepage can see the whole batch as queued/receiving/completed rather than only the currently uploading file.

During a multi-file batch, after file 1 completes but file 2 or later is still queued or uploading, the APK service homepage remains in the receiving/uploading state and must not switch to transfer-complete.
Only after every file in the current batch completes should the APK service homepage switch to transfer-complete.
Single-file upload behavior remains unchanged: the homepage may switch to transfer-complete after the single file finishes.
Paused, failed, unsupported, or over-4GB files do not cause a false transfer-complete state while valid batch files are still pending.

handoffQuestions: Explain whether the fix creates all upload sessions at batch start, introduces an explicit batch id, or uses another mechanism.
Describe exactly how the APK homepage determines receiving versus completed after this change.
List any remaining real-device validation gap.
